description: You have just identified and fixed an overly-verbose production logging issue in the fictitious service located at /home/user/app. 1. The current service version is recorded in /home/user/app/version.txt and presently contains a single line with the string 1.4.2. 2. The existing human-readable change log is /home/user/app/CHANGELOG.md. The file already contains two entries, shown here verbatim: ``` ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-30 ### Fixed - Improved timeout handling for external API requests. ## [1.4.1] - 2023-08-12 ### Changed - Upgraded Alpine base image from 3.17 to 3.18. ``` Task ---- As a DevOps engineer you must bump the patch version, because the hot-fix does not alter public APIs. Perform the following in the **simplest single shell command you can think of** (pipelines are allowed but should be one command line): a. Replace the contents of /home/user/app/version.txt with the next semantic version, 1.4.3 (newline at end of file is required). b. Prepend a brand-new section to the very top of /home/user/app/CHANGELOG.md **exactly** in the template shown below. Do not modify the older entries. Required template (the automated grader does a byte-for-byte comparison, including spaces and blank lines): ``` ## [1.4.3] - 2023-09-14 ### Fixed - Debug log verbosity reduced to INFO in production mode. ``` • There must be one blank line after the “Fixed” bullet. • After that blank line, the previous “## [1.4.2] …” header must start unchanged, so the beginning of CHANGELOG.md shall look like: ``` ## [1.4.3] - 2023-09-14 ### Fixed - Debug log verbosity reduced to INFO in production mode. ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-30 ### Fixed - Improved timeout handling for external API requests. … ``` Verification ------------ 1. /home/user/app/version.txt must contain exactly 1.4.3 followed by a single newline, nothing else. 2. The first 5 non-empty lines of /home/user/app/CHANGELOG.md must match the snippet given above byte-for-byte. 3. No additional files are to be created. Hint: A single sed, printf, or echo pipeline that edits both files is acceptable. Keep it to **one compound command line**.
